name: Salmon Croquettes with Remoulade Sauce
recipeIngredient: salt and freshly ground pepper -- to taste
salt and freshly ground pepper -- to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Make Remoulade sauce: In a small bowl, combine the mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, whole-grain mustard, dried tarragon, hot pepper sauce, capers, parsley, green onion, salt, and pepper. Set aside, covered, in the refrigerator.
2. Carefully flake the salmon into a bowl, discarding any small bones, cartilage, and skin. Add the onion, celery, 1/2 teaspoon dried tarragon, salt, and pepper; fold together with a rubber spatula.
3. Combine 1/2 cup mayonnaise with 1 tablespoon Dijon mustard in a separate bowl. Mix this into the salmon mixture.
4. With a rubber spatula, fold in the egg and 1/3 cup of cracker crumbs.
5. Form the mixture into 8 patties and coat each patty with the remaining cracker crumbs. Refrigerate, loosely covered, for 1 hour.
6. Meanwhile, coarsely grate the carrots into a large bowl. Toss with 1/3 cup of Remoulade sauce using a fork to break up the clumps.
7. Heat 1 tablespoon each of oil and butter in a nonstick skillet over medium heat. Cook the croquettes until golden, about 3 to 4 minutes per side, adding more oil and butter if necessary. Serve topped with a dollop of Remoulade sauce and the carrots alongside.
